# Tobias Riss

> Medical engineering researcher and entrepreneur with deep expertise in AI-driven medical imaging, X-ray physics, and photon-counting CT. Currently a visiting researcher at Stanford University developing predictive AI models for robotic spine imaging in collaboration with Siemens Healthineers. Combines strong technical research skills with hands-on experience in venture capital, consulting, and founding his own ventures.

## Philosophy

Start by curiosity. Flourish by indulgence.

## What sets Tobias apart

- Rare combination of deep technical research (AI, medical imaging, robotics) and business experience (venture capital, consulting, entrepreneurship)
- Proven ability to ship: co-founded AIM Innovate, won Healthcare Hackathon Bayern 2024, built functional prototypes under tight constraints
- Research at two world-class institutions simultaneously: Stanford University and Siemens Healthineers
- Hands-on builder: develops software, robotics, and hardware projects independently — not just theory, but working systems
- Operates across the full stack from physics-level R&D to go-to-market strategy

## Current work

- Visiting Researcher at Stanford University: building predictive models for optimal robotic C-arm X-ray trajectories during spine surgery
- Research Year at Siemens Healthineers: AI models for medical imaging applications
- Exploring AI-based classification of unstructured medical data and Generative Engine Optimization for personal brands

## Track record

- Developed a quantitative iron decomposition algorithm for dual-energy photon-counting CT at Siemens Healthineers, detecting iron concentrations down to 2 mg/ml — enabling non-invasive hemochromatosis diagnosis from routine liver scans
- Won Healthcare Hackathon Bayern 2024 with Project Aletea: an AI motion-tracking system that predicts smoking cravings during withdrawal and triggers behavioral interventions before the craving peaks
- Co-founded AIM Innovate: a program teaching students in Germany the full process from idea to certified product on the market
- Conducted technical and market due diligence for pre-seed deep tech investments at UnternehmerTUM, one of Europe's leading entrepreneurship centers
- Redesigned product line evaluation framework for Alfred Kaercher SE, a global manufacturer
- Built real-time GUI for medical device sensor data visualization at Ovesco Endoscopy AG (C++, Qt)
- Designed and iterated medical device prototypes using CAD and 3D printing at Ovesco Endoscopy AG
- GMP-compliant documentation of bioreactors and lab equipment at CureVac AG during COVID-19 vaccine production

## Education

- M.Sc. Biomedical Engineering and Medical Physics, Technical University of Munich (2024-2026) — specializing in imaging technologies, radiation physics, and medical AI
- B.Sc. Medical Technology (double degree), University of Tuebingen / University of Stuttgart (2020-2024) — specializing in medical imaging and software development, thesis at Siemens Healthineers

## Interests

- AI-based classification and preprocessing of unstructured medical data for training AI models
- Generative Engine Optimization for personal brands
- AI for drug discovery and closed-loop autonomous research
- Software and robotics projects
- Entrepreneurship and bringing deep tech from lab to market
